# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create Kong API Gateway configuration using declarative YAML. Define services and routes for microservices endpoints. Set up upstream targets and load balancing. This is for our API management platform.
# Category: kong_no_auth

```yaml
apiVersion: gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1alpha2  # version of Kubernetes Gateway resource type, you should match your cluster's apiVersion (default to v1beta1) if applicable in the following examples; set it at configuration creation time by --publish-not-ready status for example
kind: APIService
metadata:
  name: apis.mycompany.com   # change this with a real domain or your project's custom DNS entry (if required), e.g., myclusterapis, kubernetes180536_default(this is default for production clusters) if not set by environment variables; also used as the base path of API endpoints in HTTP requests
spec:  # replace these values with your own from env vars or defaults (if provided). For example specify this https://github.com/Kong/kubernetes-ingress-controller and kubectl versions if applicable, otherwise default to latest; also used for declaring the API version format
   group: networking.gateway  # must match your APIService's kind (in above example) at configuration creation time by --publish-not-ready status or set this value in a specific way when you create resources like kubectl apply command and specify them beforehand if needed, otherwise default to none
   version: v1alpha2  # must match your APIService's kind (in above example) at configuration creation time by --publish-not-ready status or set this value in a specific way when you create resources like kubectl apply command and specify them beforehand if needed, otherwise default to none
   route:    # list of routes definitions here. The order matters since the request is routed according to these orders; each service should be listed once per microservice endpoints setup as a single Route object in your YAML file cannot define multiple services (e.g., http://localhost/api) nor it can handle different HTTP methods
     - method: GET    # must match the actual request's verb specified when you create this route, e.g., POST or DELETE; if not provided at creation time by --publish-not-ready status then default to all supported verbs like 'GET', but only one per Route is allowed in Kong API Gateway
       path: /api   # must match the actual request's URL specified when you create this route, e.g., '/users'; if not provided at creation time by --publish-not-ready status then default to all supported verbs like 'GET', but only one per Route is allowed in Kong API Gateway
       plugins:   # list of plugin names here (only get request and cors are enabled)  this should be a simple string for each, or if you have defined them more explicitly within the routes.env config map at configuration creation time by --publish-not-ready status; also used as configurations to add HTTP requests logging
         - name: request-logger   # must match with plugins names declared in above (in first example) beforehand and set it if required, or default not provided else will use the plugin defaults like cors enabled. Enable/Disable by providing boolean values for --enable-plugins switch when kubectl apply command
       service: myservice   # must match with your microservices's name specified in config map at configuration creation time (in above example) and set it if required, or default not provided else will use the Kubernetes services defaults like hello world. Enable/Disable by providing boolean values for --enable-plugins switch when kubectl apply command
     - method: POST   # must match with actual request's verb specified at creation time (in above example) and set it if required, or default to none else will use the plugin defaults like cors enabled. Enable/Disable by providing boolean values for --enable-plugins switch when kubectl apply command
       path: /data   # must match with actual request's URL specified at creation time (in above example) and set it if required, or default to none else will use the plugin defaults like cors enabled. Enable/Disable by providing boolean values for --enable
